subject: fixing invalid portal db images
commit_message:
fixing invalid portal db images

Finding strange issues with image pulling and names subst.
When using registry.hub.docker.com and an Always pull policy
the Job pod would sit there pulling images forever even though
they are actually up to date and present.
Therefore reverting back to plain old oomk8s/blah

Issue-ID: OOM-746

Change-Id: Iec5de0e1314ac85fa4e8de6599922f1e0fd0e367
Signed-off-by: Mandeep Khinda <mandeep.khinda@amdocs.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1:

I see errors in portal-db-job:
[root@kubernetes-master-borislavto-12968-03-28-18 kubernetes]# k logs -n onap          aaa-portal-db-config-j1zlm -c portal-db-oom-update-job
+ mysql -vv --user=root --password=Aa123456 --host=aaa-portal-db --port=3306
ERROR 1049 (42000) at line 1: Unknown database 'portal'

Patch Set 1:

The job consistently fails:
onap      aaa-portal-db-config-lpxlz   0/2       Init:0/1   0         5s
onap      aaa-portal-db-config-lpxlz   0/2       PodInitializing   0         7s
onap      aaa-portal-db-config-lpxlz   0/2       Completed   0         21s
onap      aaa-portal-db-config-2d967   0/2       Pending   0         0s
onap      aaa-portal-db-config-2d967   0/2       Pending   0         0s
onap      aaa-portal-db-config-2d967   0/2       Init:0/1   0         0s
onap      aaa-portal-db-config-lpxlz   0/2       Completed   0         22s

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

You may want to allow images to come from different repos, e.g., the readiness-check coming from nexus3.onap.org vs mariadb coming from an ONAP-external repo.

Nit: It'd make sense to avoid names of specific repos (e.g. DockerHub) in the naming of variables.

By coincidence, I also tried to address this today (https://gerrit.onap.org/r/#/c/42263/), so we can probably abandon my patch.

Patch Set 2:

Borislav, Check that you don't have an existing portal database in your k8s cluster data directory.  The portal DB name went from portal_os to portal a few weeks ago.
This comes up for me on a clean system.
Frank, w.r.t. to image repos etc. it is really inconsistent across all charts right now.  The OOM team is planning on addressing this so image repositories configuration allows for much better customization.
Ideally, a repository is configured as a proxy so that all charts point to 1 and if its not there it gets pull from dockerhub and then cached.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+2 Verified+1

Mandeep, I am pretty sure I had /dockerdata-nfs clean because I use a script to cleanup my environment before deploying. Anyway, gave it another try and this time it succeeded, although with one restart of DB and the job.
